summary: Includes a generic quality criterion to verify weak rubric rejection. [CONFIG]

frontmatter.name: generic-quality-review
frontmatter.description: Use when validating that generic quality criteria do not pass a deterministic skill design review.

guiding_principle: Quality criteria must be concrete enough to verify against outputs and evidence. [DOC]
quality_criteria: The skill creates high quality output. [CONFIG]
Each criterion includes a measurable acceptance condition. [CONFIG]
Each criterion is traceable to skill behavior. [DOC]
The expected failure is the generic phrase. [CODE]
anti_patterns: Using vague adjectives as acceptance criteria.
Omitting evidence tags from rubric items.
Accepting quality language that cannot be tested.
Confusing user value with validation criteria.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Still fail because one generic item remains. [CODE]
Do not let evidence tags excuse vague criteria. [DOC]

validation: This fixture must fail on generic quality wording. [CODE]
risks: If accepted, vague rubrics could pass as deterministic criteria. [ASSUMPTION]
